name: Lahiguera, Andújar y Arjona — circular desde Villanueva de la Reina
region_intro: Road cycling around Villanueva De La Reina offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by the plains of the Guadalquivir River and the foothills leading towards the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park. The region features a mix of agricultural landscapes, historical towns, and gentle ascents. Road cyclists can expect routes that traverse open countryside and follow riverbanks, providing diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva De La Reina The most popular road cycling route is Andujar - tour around Puerto de La Cadena - Parque Natural Sierra de Andujar, a 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route winds through natural park landscapes and includes a climb over Puerto de La Cadena. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de Espeluy y Torre del Homenaje de Mengíbar — circular ribera del Río Guadalquivir, a moderate 36.2 miles (58.3 km) path. This route features historical landmarks and follows the banks of the Guadalquivir River. Local road cyclists also love the From Bailén to Castillo de Burgalimar — circular, a 14.6 miles (23.5 km) trail leading through rural roads and historical sites like Castillo de Burgalimar, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva De La Reina is defined by river plains, agricultural fields, and historical towns, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Villanueva De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Reina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva De La Reina?
A: There are 27 road cycling routes available around Villanueva De La Reina on komoot, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275095444/from-bailen-to-castillo-de-burgalimar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Bailén to Castillo de Burgalimar — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.6-mile trail that takes about 1 hour 8 minutes, leading through rural roads and historical sites.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are 5 difficult road cycling routes for experienced riders. These routes often feature more significant elevation gains and longer distances, providing a good challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: Road cycling around Villanueva De La Reina offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by the plains of the Guadalquivir River and the foothills leading towards the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park. You can expect a mix of agricultural landscapes, historical towns, and gentle ascents.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275071428/castillo-de-espeluy-y-torre-del-homenaje-de-mengibar-circular-ribera-del-rio-guadalquivir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Espeluy y Torre del Homenaje de Mengíbar — circular ribera del Río Guadalquivir&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 36.2-mile circular path featuring historical landmarks and following the banks of the Guadalquivir River.

Q: What historical sites can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Several routes pass by historical sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3144645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Baños de la Encina (Burgalimar Castle)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6700562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Cristo del Llano&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3097037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortín de Migaldías&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275095444/from-bailen-to-castillo-de-burgalimar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Bailén to Castillo de Burgalimar — circular&lt;/a&gt; route specifically highlights the castle.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1278398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pastores Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rumblar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, providing picturesque spots to pause during your ride.

Q: What natural attractions can be found near the road cycling routes?
A: The area features natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinarejo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4008210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rumblar Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also traverse parts of the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Villanueva De La Reina?
A: The road cycling routes in Villanueva De La Reina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often appreciate the varied terrain, from river plains to historical towns, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there routes that explore multiple towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes connect several towns. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275129985/lahiguera-andujar-y-arjona-circular-desde-villanueva-de-la-reina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lahiguera, Andújar y Arjona — circular desde Villanueva de la Reina&lt;/a&gt; route offers a comprehensive tour through multiple localities, showcasing the regional landscape and culture.

Q: Which route offers a good mix of natural park landscapes and climbs?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996078062/andujar-tour-around-puerto-de-la-cadena-parque-natural-sierra-de-andujar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Andujar - tour around Puerto de La Cadena - Parque Natural Sierra de Andujar&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 25.1-mile route winds through natural park landscapes and includes a climb over Puerto de La Cadena, offering both scenic beauty and a physical challenge.
tips: Nucleus with a long history behind it since it dates back to the Roman era since it is located very close to the banks of the Río Guadalquivir itself.
The history of Arjona is as rich as its cultural and monumental heritage that overlooks the Guadalquivir Valley from a gentle hill.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of the Nativity of Our Lady, Villanueva de la Reina
